designSystem.name: Cool Blue Dashboard System
designSystem.description: Airy dashboard UI with a cool-toned canvas (~#F4F7FA family), vibrant blue as brand primary (navigation pill, links, headings), pure white cards with soft lift shadows, and multi-hue stat badges (sky, teal, lavender, pink, magenta). Reference visual language: sports/analytics dashboard — crisp, calm, low visual noise.

designPrinciples.mood: Calm, optimistic, premium-but-friendly. Avoids corporate sterility.
designPrinciples.contrast: Deliberately low. Text-on-background contrast is soft; the eye is guided by spacing, color hue shifts, and gradient pools rather than hard borders or heavy weights.
designPrinciples.density: Low to medium. Generous padding inside cards, generous gaps between cards. The layout breathes.
designPrinciples.decoration: Minimal but present — one or two accent gradients, occasional 3D rendered illustration as a hero element. No skeuomorphism, no heavy shadows, no glassmorphism.
designPrinciples.emphasis: Achieved through (1) a single saturated blue gradient CTA or banner, (2) large numeric values in stat tiles, (3) the active nav item filled with the primary blue gradient. Everything else recedes.

colorSystem.strategy: Cool neutral canvas with slight blue tint; vibrant medium blue as the primary anchor; magenta/pink family as accent for segmented bars and markers. Page background reads as one continuous soft grey-blue field — cards are pure white (#FFFFFF) and separate via shadow only.

background.description: Soft horizontal/diagonal gradient from cool blue-grey through main app grey (#F4F7FA) to a slightly cooler blue-grey on the right — reads as one airy canvas.

primary.name: Vibrant Blue

gradient.usage: Active sidebar pill, primary buttons, inline links, promotional banner gradient

primary.usage: Brand and primary actions — one strong blue gradient region per major layout band (e.g. active nav + one hero card).

accent.name: Magenta Pink
accent.usage: Last segment of multi-segment progress bars, versus markers, optional highlight chips

surface.description: Cards are pure white or near-white with very high opacity. They float over the page gradient and rely on a soft shadow rather than a border to separate from the background.

text.description: Primary body copy is charcoal; labels and secondary UI text use medium grey (#707070). Tertiary for table headers and quiet metadata.

statTileAccents.description: Each stat tile uses a circular icon badge filled with a unique pastel hue. The badges are color-coded by category, creating a soft rainbow across the dashboard without overwhelming it.
statTileAccents.rule: Use one badge color per stat category. Never repeat a color in adjacent tiles.

semantic.note: Semantic hues stay saturated enough for status recognition while remaining adjacent to the cool blue UI.

progressBar.description: Horizontal segmented bar: saturated blue, light blue, neutral grey, then magenta accent — rounded caps; segments may abut for a continuous pill strip.

fontFamily.primary: A geometric humanist sans-serif. Plus Jakarta Sans, Inter, DM Sans, or Manrope are appropriate. The face must have a friendly 'a' and 'g' and read warm rather than technical.

display.usage: Page title only. One per screen.

h2.usage: Hero card headline (e.g. CTA card title)

statValue.usage: The big number in a stat tile. This is the visual anchor of the tile.

statLabel.usage: Tiny all-caps label above the stat value

tableHeader.usage: Column headers in data tables — kept very small and quiet

link.usage: 'View all' style inline links — always paired top-right of a card header

rules: Page title is the only display-sized text. Subordinate hierarchy uses size jumps of roughly 1.4x.
All-caps is reserved for stat labels and never used in body or headings.
Numeric values (stat values, table cells, scores) should sit slightly tighter than body text via letter-spacing -0.02em.
Avoid italics. Avoid underlines except on hovered links.

spacing.philosophy: Whitespace is a primary design material in this system. When in doubt, use more padding rather than less. Cards should never feel crowded against their neighbors or against their own content.

grid.columns: approx 5fr 7fr — left column narrower, right column wider
grid.alternative: Single 12-col CSS grid with content spanning 5 and 7 columns respectively
grid.rowBehavior: Cards fill their grid cell; rows align to natural content height. Avoid forcing equal heights across the row.

compositionPatterns.name: Hero pair
compositionPatterns.description: Top of dashboard: two side-by-side summary cards of equal height — one shows a featured event, the other shows aggregate stats with a progress bar.

compositionPatterns.name: Detail + stat-grid + CTA
compositionPatterns.description: Below hero: a tall data table on the left, paired with a 2x2 stat tile grid on the right, with a saturated promotional CTA card occupying the bottom-right corner.

compositionPatterns.name: Stat tile grid
compositionPatterns.description: 2-column or 4-column grid of small cards, each containing one icon badge + one tiny label + one large value.

borderRadius.rule: Border radius is generous everywhere. Nothing is sharp. Cards use lg/xl, buttons use md or pill, avatars and icon badges are always full circles.

borders.philosophy: Borders are essentially absent. Separation is done via background color shifts, soft shadows, and whitespace — not lines.
exceptions: Faint horizontal divider between table rows (1px, color #F0F1F3, optional)
Faint vertical divider in sidebar between primary nav and secondary nav (1px, color #E0E4E8, low opacity)

elevation.philosophy: Shadows are large, soft, and low-opacity — they suggest cards are floating gently above the gradient page rather than stamped onto it.
rules: Never use shadows with blur < 16px — small tight shadows look 2010-era.
Always use a slight color tint in shadow (cool blue-gray for neutral cards, primary-tinted for the CTA card).
Stat tiles use the same shadow as cards — no nested elevation.

sidebar.structure: logo (top) → primary nav group → divider → secondary nav group

active.rule: Exactly one nav item is active. The active state is the most visually prominent element in the sidebar.

iconography.style: Outline icons, 1.5-2px stroke, rounded line caps and joins. Lucide, Phosphor (regular), or Tabler are good fits.
iconography.rule: Sidebar icons are always outline — never filled, except inside the active pill where the icon may render in white outline on the gradient.

topBar.structure: greeting text (left) | flex-spacer | search-icon-button + notification-icon-button + user-pill

greeting.decoration: may include a small emoji or decorative glyph at the end

card.anatomy: header-row (title + optional inline link, right-aligned) → content-region → optional footer

headerLink.alignment: right side of header row, vertically centered with title

summaryCard.description: Top hero summary card. Contains a small meta-tag row (date/category) and a comparison or featured visual below.
summaryCard.structure: title + viewAll-link (top row) → meta row (small badge + timestamp) → main visual region (e.g. two entities + a versus marker)

versusMarker.purpose: Visually anchors a comparison between two flanking entities

statSummaryCard.description: Aggregate stats card with a horizontal multi-segment progress bar above a row of labeled metrics.
statSummaryCard.structure: title + viewAll-link → multi-segment progress bar → row of N metric columns (label above, value below)

headerRow.border: none — separation comes from color and spacing only

cellTypes.rank: small monospace-feeling number, text.tertiary, fixed width column
cellTypes.numeric: tabular numbers, text.primary, right-aligned for totals or left-aligned for individual stats
cellTypes.highlight: the rightmost 'total' or 'points' column is rendered slightly bolder (weight 600)

statTile.description: Compact metric card with one circular icon badge, a tiny uppercase label, and one large value.

iconBadge.iconColor: white or a darker tone of the same hue

statTile.label: typography.statLabel
statTile.rule: Each stat tile in a grid uses a different statTileAccents color. Never repeat in adjacent tiles.

ctaCard.description: Promotional card with saturated gradient background, prominent illustration, headline, and pill button. Acts as the visual climax of the layout.
ctaCard.structure: left column (text content) | right column (3D illustration occupying 40-50% width)

button.background: rgba(255,255,255,0.18) with subtle inner border, OR solid white with primary text

illustration.style: 3D rendered, soft-lit, isometric or three-quarter view, pastel palette matching primary+accent. Geometric primitives (spheres, cylinders, rings) on a circular podium. Renders like Spline or Blender soft-shaded.
illustration.size: fills right ~45% of card, may overflow card bounds slightly for visual interest

buttons.textLink: typography.link — no background, no padding beyond click target

iconography.style: outline, 1.5-2px stroke, rounded caps/joins, 20-24px default size
iconography.filledStyle: Used inside circular icon badges only — simple silhouette glyph in white
iconography.library: Lucide, Phosphor (Regular), Tabler, or custom in matching style
iconography.rule: Maintain a single icon family across the entire interface — do not mix outline and material/filled styles in nav or content.

motion.philosophy: Subtle, soft, never bouncy. Animations should feel like the interface is exhaling.

patterns.ctaButton: scale to 0.98 on press, scale back over 180ms
patterns.pageEnter: cards fade up with 8px translate, staggered 40ms apart

illustrations.style: 3D rendered, pastel-lit, geometric. Soft global illumination, no hard shadows, matte finish on most surfaces with one or two glossy accents.
illustrations.palette: pulled from primary + accent + at most one neutral
illustrations.subjects: abstract geometric primitives (spheres, cylinders, tori, rings, podium discs). Avoid characters, faces, or literal scene depictions.

avatars.borderTreatment: none — no ring, no border. Avatar sits cleanly on its background.
avatars.fallback: soft pastel background with monochrome initials in weight 600

logos.container: if a logo needs containing (e.g. inside a table row), wrap in a 24-28px circular surface with #FFFFFF background and elevation.card shadow

accessibility.contrastNotes: Cool grey canvas with charcoal (#1A1A1A) body copy on white cards should meet WCAG AA for normal text. Medium grey (#707070) labels — verify on tinted backgrounds. Magenta/blue segments are supplementary — pair progress bars with numeric labels.

focusRing.style: 2px solid primary.base, offset 2px, borderRadius matches element
focusRing.rule: Never remove focus rings. The default browser ring should be replaced with this branded ring, not eliminated.

accessibility.colorIndependence: Never rely on the multi-segment progress bar's hue alone to convey meaning. Pair with labels.

do: Let the page gradient breathe — keep the sidebar transparent over it.
Use exactly one filled-gradient element per major region (nav active state, CTA card).
Lean on whitespace and soft shadows for hierarchy, not borders.
Vary stat tile icon-badge colors across the grid for a soft polychromatic rhythm.
Keep numeric values large and confident; keep their labels small and quiet.
dont: Don't add hard borders or 1px dividers around cards.
Don't use saturated gradients on more than 1-2 elements per screen — they lose meaning.
Don't use pure black text or pure white shadows.
Don't mix outline and filled icons in the same region.
Don't crowd cards — if it feels tight, the answer is more padding.
